verses.1: A good name is to be more desired than great wealth; Favor is better than silver and gold.
verses.2: The rich and the poor have a common bond, The LORD is the Maker of them all.
verses.3: A prudent person sees evil and hides himself, But the naive proceed, and pay the penalty.
verses.4: The reward of humility and the fear of the LORD Are riches, honor, and life.
verses.5: Thorns and snares are in the way of the perverse; One who guards himself will be far from them.
verses.6: Train up a child in the way he should go, Even when he grows older he will not abandon it.
verses.7: The rich rules over the poor, And the borrower becomes the lender’s slave.
verses.8: One who sows injustice will reap disaster, And the rod of his fury will perish.
verses.9: One who is generous will be blessed, Because he gives some of his food to the poor.
verses.10: Drive out the scoffer, and strife will leave, Even quarreling and dishonor will cease.
verses.11: One who loves purity of heart And whose speech is gracious, the king is his friend.
verses.12: The eyes of the LORD protect knowledge, But He overthrows the words of the treacherous person.
verses.13: The lazy one says, “There is a lion outside; I will be killed in the streets!”
verses.14: The mouth of an adulteress is a deep pit; He who is cursed of the LORD will fall into it.
verses.15: Foolishness is bound up in the heart of a child; The rod of discipline will remove it far from him.
verses.16: One who oppresses the poor to make more for himself, Or gives to the rich, will only come to poverty.
verses.17: Extend your ear and hear the words of the wise, And apply your mind to my knowledge;
verses.18: For it will be pleasant if you keep them within you, So that they may be ready on your lips.
verses.19: So that your trust may be in the LORD, I have taught you today, you indeed.
verses.20: Have I not written to you excellent things Of counsels and knowledge,
verses.21: To make you know the certainty of the words of truth, So that you may correctly answer him who sent you?
verses.22: Do not rob the poor because he is poor, Nor crush the needy at the gate;
verses.23: For the LORD will plead their case And take the life of those who rob them.
verses.24: Do not make friends with a person given to anger, Or go with a hot-tempered person,
verses.25: Or you will learn his ways And find a snare for yourself.
verses.26: Do not be among those who shake hands, Among those who become guarantors for debts.
verses.27: If you have nothing with which to repay, Why should he take your bed from under you?
verses.28: Do not move the ancient boundary Which your fathers have set.
verses.29: Do you see a person skilled in his work? He will stand before kings; He will not stand before obscure people.
